channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan Celebrities Respond to Instagram Removing Fake and Inactive Followers
item.description: Kenyan celebrities have been feeling the pinch after Instagram carried out a global purge of inactive, spam, and bot accounts on May 7, 2026. The cleanup affected millions of accounts worldwide, inclu...

item.title: Labour Faces Significant Losses in Local Elections Amid Party Challenges
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer acknowledged Labour's disappointing performance in the local elections across England, describing the results as 'tough' and admitting that the losses are painful. Earl...

item.title: Ghana’s state cocoa buyer struggles to pay farmers amid debt crisis
item.description: Ghana’s state-owned cocoa buying company, Producer Buying Company (PBC), is facing a severe financial crisis, leaving it unable to purchase cocoa from farmers. The company has accumulated debts of 673...

item.title: Karachi Mayor Murtaza Wahab reaches Sindh Assembly on e-bike without helmet, pledges to follow rules
item.description: On Friday, Karachi Mayor Murtaza Wahab arrived at the Sindh Assembly riding an electric bike without wearing a helmet, which goes against the provincial government's newly implemented e-challan rules....

item.title: UK Local Election Results Confirm Fragmented Political Landscape with Reform UK Leading
item.description: According to analysis by Professor Sir John Curtice of Strathclyde University, the overnight results from the English local elections indicate that British electoral politics remains highly fragmented...

item.title: Mary Jane Veloso's Family Urges Supreme Court to Resolve Petition for Her Release
item.description: The family of Mary Jane Veloso, along with members of Migrante International, staged a protest in front of the Supreme Court in Manila to call for her immediate release. They filed a motion urging the...

item.title: Severe Tornadoes Cause Extensive Damage to Homes in Mississippi
item.description: Multiple tornadoes swept through Mississippi, causing significant damage to hundreds of homes across several communities. The storms developed rapidly, bringing destructive winds and heavy rainfall th...

item.title: Akhilesh Yadav Extends Support to TMC and DMK Amid Electoral Setbacks
item.description: On 8th May 2026, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav expressed his solidarity with the Trinamool Congress (TMC) and Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) following their electoral setbacks in the recent As...

item.title: B.C. nurse’s identity stolen in CRA hack; alleged impostor faces other charges but CRA shows limited pursuit
item.description: A British Columbia nurse named Leslie Warner had her identity stolen after her Canada Revenue Agency account was hacked in 2020. The impostor used her information to fraudulently obtain a tax refund, ...

item.title: Faith Odhiambo Criticises Karen Nyamu's Senate Apology for Remarks Made About a Visiting Grade 10 Student
item.description: Former Law Society of Kenya president Faith Odhiambo has strongly criticised nominated Senator Karen Nyamu over her apology in the Senate following inappropriate comments she made about a young female...

item.title: Afghan Special Forces Member Linked with TTP Killed in Bannu Operation
item.description: Security forces in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa’s Bannu district recently carried out a successful intelligence-based operation, resulting in the elimination of a high-profile terrorist identified as Fatehullah...

item.title: Windstorm Displaces Families and Destroys Homes in Kebbi
item.description: Na big windstorm don scatter many communities for Suru Local Government Area, Kebbi State. The storm hit on Wednesday night and affect communities like Sambera, Jeroki, Becinga, Nassarawa, Tunga Soja,...

item.title: Addressing Challenges and Employee Relations at the IDC
item.description: The Industrial Development Corporation (IDC) remains a cornerstone of South Africa's industrialisation and economic transformation, supporting entrepreneurs across all racial groups. While the publica...

item.title: Zanzibar’s First Vice President’s Office Struggles with Budget Shortfalls and Operational Challenges
item.description: Zanzibar’s Office of the First Vice President continues to face serious budget constraints that are affecting its daily operations. Acting Minister Hamza Hassan Juma presented the office’s budget prop...

item.title: NITI Aayog highlights infrastructure and teaching gaps in government schools
item.description: A recent report by NITI Aayog has revealed that many government schools in India continue to face significant infrastructural and academic challenges. Basic amenities such as electricity and clean dri...

item.title: Nigerian Army Troops Kill Three Terrorists During Operation in Zamfara State
item.description: Na so the Nigerian Army troops under Sector 2 of the Joint Task Force North-West, wey dey do Operation FANSAN YAMMA, don kill three terrorists during one fighting patrol wey dem carry out for Kaura Na...

item.title: Botswana mourns former president Festus Mogae, known for leadership and HIV/AIDS fight
item.description: Botswana has lost one of its most respected leaders as former president Festus Mogae has passed away, the government announced on Friday. Mogae, an economist by training, led the country from 1998 to ...

item.title: JUI-F stages province-wide protests in KP after Maulana Idrees is killed
item.description: PESHAWAR: The Jamiat Ulema-i-Islam-Fazl (JUI-F) conducted its second series of protest rallies across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on Friday in response to the killing of the prominent cleric Maulana Muhammad I...

item.title: John Cena Teases Major WWE Announcement Ahead of Backlash Event
item.description: John Cena, the WWE superstar whose in-ring career concluded in December after a loss to Gunther, is set to appear at the Backlash premium live event in Tampa, Florida. Cena has been actively involved ...

item.title: Mumbai Family Dies Due to Zinc Phosphide Poisoning, Investigation Underway
item.description: In Mumbai, four members of the Dokadia family tragically died within hours of each other after consuming watermelon at their home in Pydhonie. Initially suspected to be food poisoning, the case took a...
